commands.review_summary:
This PR renames the approval prompt markdown RFC from 0005 to 0002, resolves its open design questions, renames three parked RFC files, and adds accepted/issue frontmatter to those changed files.

Reproducibility: yes. Compare the latest PR head frontmatter against `README.md` and `rfcs/0000-template.md` on current main; the accepted-with-blank-issue fields and accepted parked documents are directly visible.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer or contributor needs to supply implementation issue URLs or decide the intended lifecycle state for parked RFCs; automation should not invent those policy choices.

target.title: RFC: renumber Approval Prompt Markdown to 0002, resolve questions, align frontmatter
target.body:
Per @kevinlin's request in maintainer-discussion, this renumbers the **Approval Prompt Markdown Contract** RFC from `0005` to `0002`, resolves its open questions, and cleans up frontmatter left inconsistent after the `needs_refactoring` reorg.

### Renumber + resolved questions (`0002-approval-prompt-markdown.md`)
- `0005-approval-prompt-markdown.md` → `0002-approval-prompt-markdown.md`. No other files referenced the old name.
- The three previously **Unresolved questions** are now decisions, reflected in the Proposal:
  1. **Underline** — excluded from the canonical subset; core stays at the strict cross-channel intersection and never emits underline (iMessage may map a channel-local marker out of band).
  2. **Capability shape** — a field, `approvalText`, on the existing channel approval capability object, declared via the public plugin SDK, defaulting to `"plaintext"`. No standalone capability or parallel registration path.
  3. **Exec fence language hints** — a small fixed advisory set (`sh` / none-or-`txt`); channels may honor a hint or drop an unsupported one safely without altering the fenced content.

### Parked-folder cleanup (`needs_refactoring/`)
After the June 5 reorg, the parked RFCs still held canonical numbers. Vacated them so the folder is uniformly number-free (they'll get fresh numbers when re-proposed):
- `0002-doctor-health-upgrades.md` → `doctor-health-upgrades.md`
- `0003-policy-conformance.md` → `policy-conformance.md`
- `0004-imessage-channel-configuration-cleanup.md` → `imessage-channel-configuration-cleanup.md`

### Frontmatter alignment to the new lifecycle
Added the `status` / `issue` keys (modeled on `0007`) to every RFC missing them: the three parked RFCs, plus `0001-lts-release-policy` and `0006-feeds`. All set `status: accepted` (they're merged/on `main`) with `issue:` blank. Left existing `rfc_pr` values untouched (including the `TBD` on `0001`/`0006`).

> Note: the approval RFC originally merged via #4 as `0005`; this PR amends it in place. `rfc_pr` is left pointing at the original #4.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: omarshahine
